zh.Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Franc: 法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德可米內斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort於1475年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德可米內斯時，他的父親Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II40歲，母親Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines22歲。他和Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1536年9月在他的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Franc: 法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德可米內斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort于1475年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德可米內斯时，他的父亲Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II40岁，母亲Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines22岁。他和Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1536年9月在他的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Commines
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt.
English: variant of   Cummings  .

variants.name: Cummings
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): variant of Cumming , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 岑, referring to ‘small high hills’ or ‘precipice’ in Chinese: (i) from the placename Cen (岑), the name of a fief (located in Hancheng in present-day Shaanxi province), which was granted to Qu, nephew of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ), by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (ii) for some families, this surname can be traced back to certain minority ethnic groups in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Roncq
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Amedeo, Dante, Gino, Guido, Luciano, Ottavio, Sandro, Stefano.

Italian: habitational name from any of the places called Ronca, for example in Verona province (see Ronco ), or from ronca ‘schythe’ applied probably as a metonymic occupational name for a woodcutter or mower.

variants.name: Ronco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gervais, Lucienne, Pierre.

Italian (Naples and Apulia): topographic name from ronco ‘cultivated land, terraced slope, hillside vineyard’, or a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word, examples of which are found in Turin, Genoa, Milan, and Verona. This surname is also found in southern France.

nameMeanings.name: Graaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

variants.name: Graf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Graeff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (Gräff): see Graff .
Dutch: variant of   Graef  .

variants.name: Greff
meanings: German: variant of   Graff  .

variants.name: Groff
meanings: Americanized form of German   Graff  .
Americanized form of Hungarian and Slovak Gróf, Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Grof ‘count’. The surname Groff is also found in Hungary, where it is very rare.
Americanized form of Norwegian Grov: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so called, from Old Norse grǫf ‘hollow, depression’.

variants.name: Graffius
meanings: German: Latinized (humanistic) form of   Graff  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.

He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort was born in 1475, in Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II, was 40 and his mother, Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines, was 22. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes. He died in September 1536, in his hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort est né en 1475, à Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes. Il est décédé en septembre 1536, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort 1475 in Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II 40 und seine Mutter Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines 22. Er hatte mit Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb im September 1536 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort è nato il nel 1475 nel Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes. Morì nel settembre 1536, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Franc: フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードコミンス
ja.lifeSummary: George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoortが1475年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードコミンスで生まれた時，父Johann van Halewijn de Roulers IIは40歳，母Jeanne de La Clyte de Comminesは22歳であった。彼はAntoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmesとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1536年9月に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort(이)가 1475년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 코민스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1536년 9월 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort nasceu em 1475, em Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes. Ele faleceu em setembro de 1536, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort родился в 1475 г. в местности комаинесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines, 22. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes. Он умер сентябрь 1536 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Heer van Halewijn, Commines en Roncq Graaf van Nieuwpoort nació en 1475, en Comines, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Johann van Halewijn de Roulers II, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Jeanne de La Clyte de Commines, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Antoinette de Sainte Aldegonde Noircarmes. Murió en septiembre de 1536, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
